aadya_pitani.name: Aadya Pitani
aadya_pitani.summary:
Representing Virginia, Aadya Pitani is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-2 finish at Selection places Aadya in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Aadya is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (6 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Aadya competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 3 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-2 (U13 GS, U13 GD) • Junior Nationals champion (XD U15) • 8 ORC titles.

Aadya has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 18 different players across the career:
  • Kimaya Krishna (current partner) — currently teaming up in GD (2 events, best: #5)
  • Diya Shetty — 3 events in GD/U17, best: #3, spanning 3 seasons

Having moved up to U19, Aadya is navigating tougher competition at the higher age group. The adjustment is natural — and with the pedigree Aadya brings, a return to the top is a matter of when, not if.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Aadya has competed across U13, U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Aadya has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Aadya has been seeded #1 or #2 in 9 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

Over 6 seasons and 9 titles, Aadya has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

alaina_benjamin.name: Alaina Benjamin
alaina_benjamin.summary:
Hailing from Virginia, Alaina Benjamin is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-4 finish at Selection places Alaina in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Notably, Alaina has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Alaina especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting 2 singles titles, 4 doubles titles, 5 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-4 (U13 XD) • Junior Nationals top-3 (XD U13, GD U13) • 2 ORC titles.

Beyond the trophies, the numbers stand out: Alaina boasts 22 three-game battles — proving Alaina is no stranger to the pressure of a decider, 44 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a commanding 74% win rate across 76 matches.

With 23 different doubles partners over the years, Alaina adapts to anyone:
  • Arnav Satish (current primary partner) — 5 events, 1 title together in U13/XD, spanning 3 seasons
  • Mizuki Ikeuchi (current primary partner) — 3 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (3 total events in GD, across 2 seasons)
  • Medha Sivani Gudavalli (current partner) — an ongoing partnership that keeps producing results: 3 titles across 7 events in GD, across 2 seasons
  • Kevin Xie (current partner) — 2 events, 1 title together in XD, across 2 seasons

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 3 titles across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Alaina is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Alaina has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Alaina has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 6 seeded entries across the career, Alaina consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 11 titles, Alaina is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

alice_luo.name: Alice Luo
alice_luo.summary:
Representing California, Alice Luo has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U15 level, breaking into the semifinals 1 time. Across 45 entries in 16 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

Notably, Alice has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Alice competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a doubles best of #4. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (3 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (9 entries).

Alice has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 11 different players across the career:
  • Logan Lu (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in U15/XD (6 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Riya Naik — 3 events in GD/U15, best: #4

A veteran of the junior circuit, Alice has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Alice has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience, Alice brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

alice_meng.name: Alice Meng
alice_meng.summary:
A California competitor, Alice Meng is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-3 finish at Selection places Alice in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Alice is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (5 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Alice competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 4 singles titles, 6 doubles titles, 5 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-3 (U13 GD, U15 GD) • Junior Nationals champion (XD U13) • 14 ORC titles.

Beyond the trophies, the numbers stand out: Alice boasts an extraordinary 80% match win rate across 70 matches, 38 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a career +547 point differential.

Alice has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 15 different players across the career:
  • Jacob Zhou (current primary partner) — 9 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (12 total events in U15/XD, spanning 3 seasons)
  • Sophia Li (current partner) — 2 events, 1 title together in GD
  • Lexuan Wen (current partner) — 1 events, 1 title together in GD
  • Annie Meng (current partner) — 1 events, 1 title together in GD

Alice is peaking at the right time in 2025-2026, with 6 titles across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Alice is still ascending.

Alice has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Alice has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 14 seeded entries across the career, Alice consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 6 seasons and 15 titles, Alice has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

arnav_satish.name: Arnav Satish
arnav_satish.summary:
Representing Massachusetts, Arnav Satish is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-4 finish at Selection places Arnav in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Notably, Arnav has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Arnav is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 2 singles titles, a doubles best of #3, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-4 (U13 XD) • Junior Nationals top-3 (XD U13) • 1 ORC title.

The statistics paint a vivid picture — Arnav boasts a career +554 point differential, a biggest win margin of 32 points, a solid 67% win rate over 91 matches.

Arnav has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 17 different players across the career:
  • Joey Wang (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in BD/U13 (7 events, best: #3, across 2 seasons)
  • Alaina Benjamin (current primary partner) — 5 events, 1 title together in U13/XD, spanning 3 seasons
  • Vindhya Chekuru (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, across 2 seasons)

Arnav is peaking at the right time in 2025-2026, with 1 title across 5 tournaments — a clear sign that Arnav is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Arnav has competed across U11, U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Arnav has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 3 titles, Arnav is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Hailing from California, Aryan Dayaram has stepped onto the championship stage, currently competing at the U19 level, reaching the finals 1 time. With 27 entries across 13 tournaments, Aryan is proving the ability to compete at the highest level.

Aryan is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (1 entry) • Junior Nationals competitor (4 entries).

With 12 different doubles partners over the years, Aryan adapts to anyone:
  • Audrey Zhao (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, across 2 seasons)

Aryan has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Aryan has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Aryan brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

aryan_mohan.name: Aryan Mohan
aryan_mohan.summary:
A California competitor, Aryan Mohan broke through with a tournament title, currently competing at the U17 level, a moment that separates contenders from champions. With 43 entries across 22 tournaments, the competitive foundation is built — and more titles are within reach.

Notably, Aryan has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Aryan is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #4, 1 doubles title, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (2 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (10 entries).

With 12 different doubles partners over the years, Aryan adapts to anyone:
  • Nidhi Sujin (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (4 events, best: #2, spanning 3 seasons)
  • Alvin Feng Heng Ho (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in BD/U17 (3 events, across 2 seasons)

Aryan has been on this journey through U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Aryan has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 6 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title, Aryan is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Ashley is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-2 (U19 GD, Mixed Doubles) • Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries) • ORC top-2 finisher.

Ashley has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 6 different players across the career:
  • Joshua J Yang (current partner) — currently teaming up in Mixed/XD (2 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)
  • Kodi Tang Lee (current partner) — currently teaming up in GD/U19 (2 events, best: #2)
  • Isaac Yang (current partner) — currently teaming up in U19/XD (2 events, best: #4)

With tournaments spanning NorCal, SoCal, South, Ashley isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Ashley's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

Notably, Atulith has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Atulith competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

Atulith has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Atulith isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

audrey_chang.name: Audrey Chang
audrey_chang.summary:
Hailing from California, Audrey Chang has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U19 GD, U19 GS, U15 GD, U17 GD, Mixed Doubles, U19 XD, Women's Singles, U17 GS, U17 XD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 15 career titles across 23 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Audrey is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (11 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Audrey is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 6 singles titles, 4 doubles titles, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U19 GD, U19 GS, U15 GD, U17 GD, Mixed Doubles, U19 XD, Women's Singles, U17 GS, U17 XD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals top-2 (GS U19, XD U17) • 5 ORC titles.

With 12 different doubles partners over the years, Audrey adapts to anyone:
  • Tian Qi Alan Zhang (current primary partner) — 4 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (5 total events in Mixed/U19/XD, across 2 seasons)
  • Jasmine Yeung (current primary partner) — 3 events together this season alone with 3 titles, building serious momentum (3 total events in GD/U19)
  • Kyle Wang — 2 events in XD, best: #2
  • Youshi Chen — a trusted U17/XD partner (7 events, best: #3, across 2 seasons)

The 2024-2025 season has been a step up, with 5 titles across 5 tournaments — a clear sign that Audrey is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Audrey has competed across U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Audrey has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Audrey has been seeded #1 or #2 in 8 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 15 titles, Audrey is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

What makes Ayaaz especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting 3 singles titles, 3 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (9 entries) • Junior Nationals champion (BS U15) • ORC top-2 finisher.

Beyond the trophies, the numbers stand out: Ayaaz boasts 57 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a commanding 71% win rate across 103 matches, a clutch 52% win rate in three-game matches (12/23).

Ayaaz has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 27 different players across the career:
  • Haziqa H Mathani (current primary partner) — 4 events together this season alone with 2 titles, building serious momentum (4 total events in XD, across 2 seasons)
  • Mihika Jaiswal (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (4 events, best: #2)
  • Cayden Wang (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in BD (3 events, best: #4)
  • Anthony Cheng Xu (current partner) — 3 events, 1 title together in BD, across 2 seasons

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 10 podium finishes across 7 tournaments. Ayaaz is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Ayaaz has competed across U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Ayaaz has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 13 seeded entries across the career, Ayaaz consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 9 titles, Ayaaz is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.
